request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will US tariffs on Chinese goods exceed 60% average effective rate in 2026?
Kind: active
Timeline (2 events):
- 2026-02-25 [background] PDF Version — Mentions Department of Commerce but is a generic 'PDF Version' with no substantive notice or Federal Register determination about tariffs >60%.
- 2026-02-25 [background] View Comments — Generic 'View Comments' entry names Dept. of Commerce but contains no tariff/China substance, so it doesn't change resolution probability.

response.text: Thus far, the record shows only generic Commerce-linked “PDF Version” and “View Comments” stubs with no substantive China/tariff content, offering no regulatory signal toward >60% average rates. No USTR/Commerce/ITC Federal Register notices, proposed rules, or proclamations indicate movement to that level. Pending triggers would be new Section 301 actions, presidential proclamations, or legislation raising average effective rates.
